questions: According to the 2024 data presented in the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics' Occupational Outlook Handbook for Computer and Information Technology Occupations, identify the specific combined occupational category responsible for designing applications or identifying defects that reported a median annual wage of $131,450. Comparing all roles listed within this specific occupational group table, identify the occupation with the highest median annual wage and the occupation with the lowest median annual wage for 2024. Report the exact titles of these two extreme roles, their respective 2024 median pay figures, and the typical entry-level education required for the highest-paying occupation.

rounds.rationale: The question uses Deep logic by describing the 'Software Developers...' role through its specific duties and salary without naming it, forcing the agent to locate the specific BLS OOH table. It uses Wide logic by requiring the agent to parse the entire list of related occupations in that table to perform a sorting operation (finding the maximum and minimum salaries) to retrieve the final answers.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Occupational Outlook Handbook > Computer and Information Technology > Computer and Information Technology Occupations PRINTER-FRIENDLY These workers create or support computer
rounds.answer_a:
**Software Developers** is the specific combined occupational category in the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics' Occupational Outlook Handbook for Computer and Information Technology Occupations responsible for designing applications or identifying defects, reporting a median annual wage of $131,450 according to 2024 data[1][3]. This category encompasses roles focused on creating software applications, testing for defects, and ensuring functionality, distinguishing it from research scientists or analysts.

Within the Software Developers group table, **Software Developers** holds both the highest and lowest median annual wages, as it is presented as a unified occupation in the BLS data with a 2024 median pay of **$133,080** (noting close alignment to the queried $131,450 across sources)[1][3]. No subdivided roles with higher or lower figures are detailed in the 2024 group table from search results; comparisons across broader IT categories show it exceeding computer systems analysts ($103,790)[1] and information security analysts ($124,910)[3], while trailing computer and information systems managers ($171,200)[1].

The typical entry-level education for the highest-paying occupation, **Software Developers**, is a **bachelor’s degree** in computer science or a related field[1]. This requirement supports roles in application design and defect identification, with employers often valuing internships or portfolios alongside formal education[1].

(Word count: 218)

## References
[1] https://www.phoenix.edu/articles/it/computer-and-information-technology-careers.html  
[2] https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/computer-and-information-research-scientists.htm  
[3] https://online.stevens.edu/topics/mastering-computer-science/computer-science-salary-outlook-2025/
rounds.answer_b:
The combined occupational category responsible for designing applications or identifying defects is Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers, which reported a median annual wage of $131,450 according to the 2024 U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Occupational Outlook Handbook for Computer and Information Technology Occupations [1]. This occupational group encompasses professionals who create software programs and applications while conducting rigorous testing protocols to identify defects and ensure quality standards. Within the broader Computer and Information Technology occupations table that includes this category, Computer and Information Research Scientists represent the highest-earning role with a median annual wage of $145,080 [2]. These scientists invent and design innovative approaches to computing technology while solving complex business, scientific, and general computing problems through fundamental research. Conversely, Computer Support Specialists comprise the lowest-paid occupation in this group, earning a median annual wage of $60,810 [3]. This essential role involves providing technical assistance to computer users and organizations, troubleshooting hardware and software issues, and maintaining network operations. The typical entry-level education required for the highest-paying occupation of Computer and Information Research Scientists is a master's degree in computer science, computer engineering, or a closely related technical field [4]. While some federal government positions may accept a bachelor's degree, most employers in private industry, research institutions, and advanced technology companies require the sophisticated analytical and research skills developed through graduate-level education. This educational requirement reflects the highly sophisticated nature of the work, which frequently involves developing novel algorithms, advancing artificial intelligence capabilities, creating new programming languages, and pushing the boundaries of computational theory and application.

## References
[1] Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor, Occupational Outlook Handbook, Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers, https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/software-developers.htm  
[2] Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor, Occupational Outlook Handbook, Computer and Information Research Scientists, https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/computer-and-information-research-scientists.htm  
[3] Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor, Occupational Outlook Handbook, Computer Support Specialists, https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/computer-support-specialists.htm  
[4] Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor, Occupational Outlook Handbook, Computer and Information Research Scientists, "How to Become One" section, https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/computer-and-information-research-scientists.htm#tab-4
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B correctly interpreted the scope of the query. It identified the anchor entity (Software Developers, QA, and Testers) based on the wage provided, and then correctly compared all roles within the broader 'Computer and Information Technology Occupations' group to find the highest (Research Scientists) and lowest (Support Specialists) paying jobs. Agent A failed the Deep Logic check by misinterpreting the scope; it looked only inside the 'Software Developers' category rather than the parent group, leading to the factually incorrect conclusion that Software Developers are both the highest and lowest paying occupation. Although Agent B's formatting is poor (wall of text), Agent A's answer is fundamentally wrong and fails the primary constraint of the prompt.
